title: Rich Uruguayan Lentil Curry
description: An earthy, pillowy curry that blends red lentils with fragrant panang paste, light coconut milk, and a splash of lime for a low-glycemic crowd-pleaser.
instructions: Rinse lentils under cold water until clear.
In a large pot, sauté onion and garlic in 1 tbsp oil until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in panang curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add lentils, water, and cumin, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 25 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and coconut cream, stir, and continue simmering for 10 minutes until lentils are tender and sauce thickened.
Stir in lime juice and adjust salt to taste.
Garnish with chopped cilantro before serving.
Serve hot over steamed quinoa or cauliflower rice if desired.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat gently on the stove or microwave before serving.
